Calcification involves deposition of calcium salts, often related to phosphate balance. Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) is known for its role in hydroxyapatite crystal formation, crucial in bone mineralization. Pyrophosphatase might also be relevant because it breaks down pyrophosphate, which can inhibit calcification. Carbonic anhydrase is more related to acid-base balance, not directly calcification.
